topic: An artist's work is displayed without their permission
raw_output:
[story 1]

The gallery lights felt like physical blows, searing my eyes as they caught the canvas. My breath hitched, a sharp, shallow intake that did nothing to steady the tremor starting in my hands. There, hanging center stage, was ‘Nocturne in Rust,’ my masterpiece. But it wasn't just hanging; it was surrounded by these other pieces, stark and unsettling, and a man in a crisp suit was gesturing towards it, his smile too wide, too knowing. My heart hammered against my ribs, a frantic drum solo in the sudden, suffocating silence of my own mind. I took a step forward, the polished floor seeming to stretch into an abyss, my throat suddenly dry and tight. "Excuse me," I managed, the sound a reedy whisper that dissolved before it reached him.

[story 2]

I froze near the entrance, my gaze locked on the exhibition. Every shadow seemed to shift, every reflected gleam seemed to mock my presence. When I saw it—my portrait, rendered in oils I poured my soul into—displayed without a single note, a cold dread washed over me, chilling me to the bone despite the warmth of the room. My palms began to sweat, slick and cold against the fabric of my jacket. I wanted to bolt, to disappear into the anonymity of the street outside, but my feet felt cemented to the marble floor. I took a shaky breath, forcing my legs to move, a clumsy, desperate shuffle toward the display, my eyes darting between the artwork and the imposing security guards.

[story 3]

The air in the room grew thick, heavy with the scent of expensive perfume and unearned admiration. I watched, utterly still, as they circled the painting, their murmurs turning into approving sighs. My stomach twisted into a tight knot, a violent clenching that made it hard to draw a full breath. This wasn't just an oversight; this was a violation, a theft of my very voice made visible. A wave of intense agitation washed over me, making my vision swim slightly at the edges. I clenched my jaw so hard my teeth ached, fighting the urge to lash out, to shatter the pristine glass separating me from my creation.

There, hanging center stage, was ‘Nocturne in Rust,’ my masterpiece. But it wasn't just hanging; it was surrounded by these other pieces, stark and unsettling, and a man in a crisp suit was gesturing towards it, his smile too wide, too knowing. My heart hammered against my ribs, a frantic drum solo in the sudden, suffocating silence of my own mind. I took a step forward, the polished floor seeming to stretch into an abyss, my throat suddenly dry and tight. "Excuse me," I managed, the sound a reedy whisper that dissolved before it reached him.

I froze near the entrance, my gaze locked on the exhibition. Every shadow seemed to shift, every reflected gleam seemed to mock my presence. When I saw it—my portrait, rendered in oils I poured my soul into—displayed without a single note, a cold dread washed over me, chilling me to the bone despite the warmth of the room. My palms began to sweat, slick and cold against the fabric of my jacket. I wanted to bolt, to disappear into the anonymity of the street outside, but my feet felt cemented to the marble floor. I took a shaky breath, forcing my legs to move, a clumsy, desperate shuffle toward the display, my eyes darting between the artwork and the imposing security guards.

The air in the room grew thick, heavy with the scent of expensive perfume and unearned admiration. I watched, utterly still, as they circled the painting, their murmurs turning into approving sighs. My stomach twisted into a tight knot, a violent clenching that made it hard to draw a full breath. This wasn't just an oversight; this was a violation, a theft of my very voice made visible. A wave of intense agitation washed over me, making my vision swim slightly at the edges. I clenched my jaw so hard my teeth ached, fighting the urge to lash out, to shatter the pristine glass separating me from my creation.
